    • Analysing bathymetric data in R with marmap

Read bathymetric data in XYZ format

Source: R/mar_read_bathy.R
mar_read_bathy.Rd

Reads a three-column table containing longitude (x), latitude (y) and depth (z) data.

Usage

mar_read_bathy(xyz, header = FALSE, sep = ",", ...)

read.bathy(xyz, header = FALSE, sep = ",", ...)

Arguments

xyz

three-column table with longitude (x), latitude (y) and depth (z) (no default)

header

whether this table has a row of column names (default = FALSE)

sep

character separating columns, (default=",")

...

further arguments to be passed to read.table()

Value

The output of mar_read_bathy is a matrix of class bathy, which dimensions depends on the resolution of the grid uploaded from the NOAA GEODAS server (Grid Cell Size). The class bathy has its own methods for summarizing and ploting the data.

See also

mar_summary_bathy, mar_plot_bathy, mar_read_gebco_bathy

Author

Eric Pante

Examples

# load NW Atlantic data
data(nw.atlantic)

# write example file to disk
write.table(nw.atlantic, "NW_Atlantic.csv", sep=",", quote=FALSE, row.names=FALSE)

# use mar_read_bathy
mar_read_bathy("NW_Atlantic.csv", header=TRUE) -> atl

# remove temporary file
system("rm NW_Atlantic.csv") # remove file, for unix-like systems

# class "bathy"
class(atl)

# summarize data of class "bathy"
summary(atl)

On this page
